site.title: AI Builder's Field Guide
site.tagline: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

categories.name: AI Coding Tools
categories.blurb: Agents and IDEs that read, write, and run your code. Where you reach when you want AI inside your editor.
categories.intent: I want AI in my editor

categories.name: Multi-Agent Coding
categories.blurb: Run, orchestrate, and embed fleets of coding agents — local, cross-device, or inside your product.
categories.intent: I want to run many coding agents at once

categories.name: AI Design Skills
categories.blurb: Reusable skills and prompts that teach coding agents about visual design.
categories.intent: I want my AI-built UIs to stop looking generic

categories.name: App Builders
categories.blurb: Generate a working full-stack app from a prompt. Prototype-to-production in minutes.
categories.intent: I want a working app from a prompt

categories.name: Hosted Frontier APIs
categories.blurb: The closed-weight frontier models you call from your stack — Claude, GPT, Gemini.
categories.intent: I need the best-available model behind an API

categories.name: Open-Weight Models
categories.blurb: Downloadable model families. Host yourself or run on any inference platform.
categories.intent: I want a model I can run on my own hardware

categories.name: Inference & Hosting
categories.blurb: Where to run open-weight models in production — managed inference, fine-tuning, custom silicon.
categories.intent: I need to host an open model at scale

categories.name: Agent Frameworks & SDKs
categories.blurb: The glue for building autonomous, tool-using agents.

categories.name: Agent Memory
categories.blurb: Persistent memory layers — what your agent knows across sessions, users, and turns.
categories.intent: My agent needs to remember things

categories.name: Retrieval & Search
categories.blurb: Vector stores, full-text search, and web search APIs. Stateless retrieval — different from memory.
categories.intent: My agent needs to look things up

categories.name: MCP Ecosystem
categories.blurb: Servers, clients, and registries for the Model Context Protocol — the standard for connecting agents to tools.
categories.intent: I need to give my agent tools

categories.name: Voice & Audio
categories.blurb: Speech synthesis, voice cloning, realtime agents, and music generation.
categories.intent: I need a voice or audio output

categories.name: AI Video
categories.blurb: Text-to-video models and programmatic video frameworks.
categories.intent: I need to generate or compose video

categories.name: AI 3D
categories.blurb: Text- or image-to-3D generators and 3D asset pipelines.
categories.intent: I need 3D assets — games, AR, products

categories.name: Evals, Benchmarks & Observability
categories.blurb: Measure what your agents and models actually do. Public leaderboards and production observability.
categories.intent: I need to know if my AI works

entries.name: Claude Code
entries.summary: Anthropic's official terminal-native coding agent — reads your repo, edits files, runs commands, talks to MCP servers.
entries.why_it_matters: The reference implementation for agentic coding: full-context repo understanding, permissions model, hooks, and skills. If you're building developer agents, study how it ships.

entries.name: Cursor
entries.summary: AI-first VS Code fork with Composer, Tab autocomplete, and a strong agent loop.
entries.why_it_matters: The dominant IDE among working AI builders in 2026 — diff review UX is the bar everyone else is chasing.

entries.name: Windsurf
entries.summary: Codeium's agentic IDE with Cascade — strong long-running task execution, now part of OpenAI.
entries.why_it_matters: Cascade pioneered the 'agent that keeps going across many turns' pattern; worth comparing against Cursor Composer for taste.

entries.name: Aider
entries.summary: Open-source CLI pair-programmer — git-native, model-agnostic, and the benchmark for many leaderboards.
entries.why_it_matters: The polyglot baseline. If you're benchmarking your own agent, Aider's leaderboards are where the field calibrates.

entries.name: Cline
entries.summary: Open-source autonomous coding agent for VS Code — plan/act modes, MCP support, BYO model.
entries.why_it_matters: The transparent option: every action shown and approvable. Good for understanding how an agent loop actually works.

entries.name: Claude API (Anthropic)
entries.summary: Anthropic's API — Claude Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5. Native tool use, prompt caching, extended thinking, files, citations.
entries.why_it_matters: Best-in-class for long-horizon tool use and agentic coding. Prompt caching alone often pays for the migration.

entries.name: OpenAI API
entries.summary: GPT-5 family, Realtime API, structured outputs, Responses API.
entries.why_it_matters: Still the broadest ecosystem and the realtime voice baseline. Responses API is the cleanest agent primitive OpenAI ships.

entries.name: Gemini API (Google)
entries.summary: Gemini 2.5 Pro / Flash with 2M-token context, native multimodal, and grounding-with-Search.
entries.why_it_matters: If your workload is video, very-long-context, or needs fresh web grounding without your own RAG, start here.

entries.name: OpenRouter
entries.summary: Single API for hundreds of models with automatic failover and unified billing.
entries.why_it_matters: The pragmatic abstraction layer when you don't want to hard-code one provider. Useful for model A/B and graceful fallback.

entries.name: Claude Agent SDK
entries.summary: Anthropic's official SDK for building agents that share Claude Code's runtime — tools, MCP, hooks, subagents.
entries.why_it_matters: If you want Claude Code's agent harness for your own product, this is the supported path. Less reinvention than building from raw API calls.

entries.name: LangGraph
entries.summary: Graph-based agent orchestration from LangChain — durable execution, human-in-the-loop, time-travel debugging.
entries.why_it_matters: When your agent has real branching state and you need to checkpoint/replay, the graph model pays off.

entries.name: Mastra
entries.summary: TypeScript-first agent framework — workflows, memory, evals, and a local dev playground.
entries.why_it_matters: The most ergonomic TS option in 2026 for builders who don't want to leave Node. Strong eval story baked in.

entries.name: Inspect (UK AISI)
entries.summary: Open-source eval framework from the UK AI Safety Institute — used for the public capability evals you read about.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're publishing eval results you want anyone to reproduce, Inspect is the lingua franca.

entries.name: Braintrust
entries.summary: Eval, logging, and prompt-playground stack used by many production AI teams.
entries.why_it_matters: The 'we ship LLM features and need to know if a prompt change broke things' tool — diffs across runs are first-class.

entries.name: Langfuse
entries.summary: Open-source LLM observability — traces, evals, prompts, self-hostable.
entries.why_it_matters: The default open-source pick for tracing agent runs. Self-host if your data can't leave the building.

entries.name: pgvector
entries.summary: Vector similarity search inside Postgres. HNSW + IVFFlat indexes, just another column type.
entries.why_it_matters: The boring-tech RAG stack: keep your embeddings next to your relational data. Most apps don't need a dedicated vector DB.

entries.name: turbopuffer
entries.summary: Serverless vector + full-text search on object storage — pay per query, scales to billions of vectors.
entries.why_it_matters: When pgvector starts groaning and you don't want to babysit a Pinecone bill, turbopuffer is the 2026 sweet spot.

entries.name: v0 by Vercel
entries.summary: Generate React/Next.js UIs from prompts — full-stack mode, git sync, deploy to Vercel in one click.
entries.why_it_matters: Highest-fidelity prompt-to-production UI in 2026 if your stack is Next + shadcn. Treat it as a fast scaffolder, not a finisher.

entries.name: Lovable
entries.summary: Prompt-to-app builder — full-stack, Supabase-backed, GitHub-synced.
entries.why_it_matters: The leading non-developer-friendly app builder. Good for testing whether an idea has shape before you spend a dev week on it.

entries.name: Bolt.new
entries.summary: StackBlitz's in-browser full-stack agent — runs Node in WebContainers, no local setup.
entries.why_it_matters: When the friction of 'clone, install, run' is the bottleneck on idea velocity, Bolt removes it entirely.

entries.name: ElevenLabs
entries.summary: TTS, voice cloning, conversational AI, and the Agents platform for realtime voice.
entries.why_it_matters: Still the quality bar for synthetic voice. The Agents platform is the fastest path from idea to a phone-callable voice agent.

entries.name: Cartesia
entries.summary: Sonic — sub-100ms streaming TTS with state-space models. The realtime-voice latency leader.
entries.why_it_matters: If your voice agent feels laggy, the model is usually the issue. Cartesia's the default fix.

entries.name: Model Context Protocol
entries.summary: Open standard for connecting LLMs to tools, data, and prompts. Now supported by Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and most agent frameworks.
entries.why_it_matters: MCP won the 'how do agents talk to tools' debate. If you're building anything an agent needs to use, ship an MCP server.

entries.name: Smithery
entries.summary: Registry and one-click installer for MCP servers — discover, install, and host MCP servers without local config.
entries.why_it_matters: The npm of the MCP ecosystem in 2026. Start here when you need an MCP server before writing one.

entries.name: MemPalace
entries.summary: Open-source local AI memory system — stores conversation history as verbatim text with semantic retrieval.
entries.why_it_matters: Hits 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval with zero API calls. If you want agent memory that doesn't leak to a vendor, this is the open-source baseline.

entries.name: OB1
entries.summary: Self-hosted shared memory database — lets multiple AI tools read and write one persistent memory without vendor lock-in.
entries.why_it_matters: Bridges the memory silos: Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT each have their own. OB1 is the memory backbone that unifies them.

entries.name: You.com Search API
entries.summary: Web search API engineered for AI agents — 300ms p99 latency, real-time indexing across 10M+ news sources.
entries.why_it_matters: When you need fresh web grounding inside an agent and Tavily or Exa feels slow, You.com's latency is the differentiator.

entries.name: DeepResearch-Bench
entries.summary: Open benchmark and leaderboard for deep-research agents (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and OSS) — hosted by muset-ai on Hugging Face Spaces.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're shipping a research agent or deciding which deep-research product to integrate, this is the public scoreboard to read first.

entries.name: Zed AI
entries.summary: Rust-native code editor with agentic editing, real-time collaboration, and the open Agent Client Protocol (ACP).
entries.why_it_matters: The performance-first alternative to Cursor and Windsurf. 120 fps render, open prediction model (Zeta), and ACP make agent behavior auditable.

entries.name: Conductor
entries.summary: Mac app that runs a fleet of Claude Code and Codex agents in parallel git worktrees, then helps you review and merge their work.
entries.why_it_matters: Single-machine multi-agent orchestration with native macOS UX. The 'one developer, many parallel agents' workflow distilled into a desktop app.

entries.name: Paseo
entries.summary: Unified runner for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and others — accessible from phone, desktop, or terminal.
entries.why_it_matters: If you switch between coding agents and devices, Paseo is the connector. Picks up where Claude.ai's mobile leaves off for power users.

entries.name: Sandcastle
entries.summary: TypeScript library by Matt Pocock for orchestrating AI coding agents in isolated sandboxes — one function call handles lifecycle and branch merging.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're building a product that spawns coding agents, Sandcastle removes the boilerplate around containers and merge UX.

entries.name: Taalas
entries.summary: Compiles AI models directly into custom silicon — model weights and parameters live on the chip, eliminating memory retrieval.
entries.why_it_matters: Their ChatJimmy demo hits ~17K tokens/sec/user vs. ~1,800 on GPUs. Hardware bet that 'the model is the computer' — worth watching if inference cost dominates your stack.

entries.name: HyperFrames
entries.summary: HeyGen's open-source HTML-based video rendering framework with first-class AI agent support (Apache 2.0).
entries.why_it_matters: Remotion needs React and a build step; HyperFrames lets an agent author videos as plain HTML. Better fit for LLM agents that already speak HTML fluently.

entries.name: Remotion
entries.summary: React-based framework for creating videos programmatically using CSS, Canvas, SVG, and WebGL.
entries.why_it_matters: The most mature programmatic-video stack. Treat video as a React app, render in CI. Source-available — commercial use needs a license.

entries.name: Tripo3D
entries.summary: Text, image, or sketch to production-ready 3D assets — texturing, rigging, and animation in one workflow.
entries.why_it_matters: The all-in-one 3D generator for game, AR, and product pipelines. Beats single-feature competitors when you need riggable output, not just a mesh.

entries.name: Hunyuan3D-2
entries.summary: Tencent's open 3D asset generator — two-stage pipeline with flow-based diffusion transformer for shape and separate texture synthesis.
entries.why_it_matters: Open weights for high-resolution textured 3D. Can also re-texture handcrafted meshes — useful when Tripo3D's hosted workflow isn't an option.

entries.name: Impeccable
entries.summary: Design skill for Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI — 23 commands to push AI-generated UIs past generic defaults.
entries.why_it_matters: Most AI-built UIs look the same because the agents lack design vocabulary. Impeccable installs that vocabulary as a reusable skill.

entries.name: Claude Design System Prompt
entries.summary: Public gist of a Claude system prompt for design and prototyping — HTML artifacts, design systems, animation, asset verification.
entries.why_it_matters: A worked example of turning a coding agent into a design partner. Steal the structure for your own design skill or compare to Impeccable.

entries.name: DeepSeek
entries.summary: Chinese AI lab shipping open-weight LLMs (R1, V3, V4-preview, Coder V2) with strong reasoning and aggressive pricing.
entries.why_it_matters: When Claude or GPT's API bill bites, DeepSeek is the open-weight escape hatch with competitive reasoning. Frequently profiled by The AI Search.

entries.name: Qwen
entries.summary: Alibaba Cloud's open-weight model family — Qwen3.6 text+multimodal, Qwen3.5 Omni (text/audio/image/video), and WebWorld agent variants.
entries.why_it_matters: The most prolific open-weight family in 2026 — base, coder, vision, audio, and agent specializations all maintained. First place to look for non-English fine-tunes.

entries.name: Gemma
entries.summary: Google DeepMind's open-weight model family — Gemma 4 at 2B/4B/26B/31B parameters, runs on consumer hardware.
entries.why_it_matters: Google's answer to Llama and Qwen. If you want an open-weight model with first-party Google tooling (Vertex, Gemini API parity), Gemma is the path.

entries.name: Groq
entries.summary: Inference provider with custom LPU silicon — open-weight model hosting at order-of-magnitude lower latency than GPU-based APIs.
entries.why_it_matters: When your agent loop is bottlenecked on per-call latency (not raw quality), Groq is the swap. Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, and Whisper all available.

entries.name: Together AI
entries.summary: Production-grade inference + fine-tuning platform for open-weight models, with proprietary speculative decoding and custom kernels.
entries.why_it_matters: OpenRouter aggregates; Together engineers the stack. Pick Together when you need to fine-tune an OSS model and serve it at scale on the same platform.

entries.name: Wan (Alibaba)
entries.summary: Alibaba's creative AI platform — text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, image editing in one suite.
entries.why_it_matters: Frequently featured by The AI Search for matching closed-model quality on video while remaining accessible. The Chinese answer to Sora and Runway.

entries.name: Mem0
entries.summary: Drop-in memory infrastructure for AI agents — automatic learning from interactions, persistent context across sessions.
entries.why_it_matters: If MemPalace and OB1 are the OSS DIY paths, Mem0 is the managed-service alternative. Faster to integrate when you don't want to host memory yourself.

entries.name: Letta (MemGPT)
entries.summary: Persistent-agent framework with continual learning — direct descendant of UC Berkeley's MemGPT research.
entries.why_it_matters: The research-backed approach to agent memory: hierarchical context, self-editing memory blocks. Different mental model from drop-in retrieval like Mem0.

entries.name: Suno v5
entries.summary: Suno v5 is an AI music generation tool that creates full-length tracks with human-like vocals across various genres.
entries.why_it_matters: This tool allows creators to produce original, royalty-free music efficiently, providing a cost-effective alternative to traditional music production.

entries.name: Baton
entries.summary: Baton is a desktop application that provides a unified interface for developing and managing multiple AI coding agents and their associated worktrees.
entries.why_it_matters: It simplifies the orchestration of several AI coding agents, offering a more organized and efficient workflow compared to manually managing them across disparate development environments.

entries.name: Statewright
entries.summary: Statewright utilizes visual state machines to enhance the reliability of AI agents by formally restricting their tool access, iteration limits, and valid transitions within a workflow.
entries.why_it_matters: This approach improves agent reliability by reducing the problem complexity for smaller models, offering an alternative to relying on larger models or extensive prompting for robust agent behavior.

entries.name: Helicone
entries.summary: Helicone provides logging, monitoring, and debugging for large language model applications to help builders understand and optimize their AI usage.
entries.why_it_matters: It offers detailed insights into LLM calls, including latency and cost, providing a more focused observability solution than broader eval platforms.

entries.name: OpenSwarm
entries.summary: OpenSwarm is an open-source multi-agent system that orchestrates specialized AI agents to create diverse deliverables from a single terminal prompt.
entries.why_it_matters: Unlike single-agent coding tools, OpenSwarm coordinates multiple specialist agents for tasks beyond code, such as research, documents, and media generation.

entries.name: Hermes Agent
entries.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source framework for building self-improving AI agents designed for long-term, personalized collaboration.
entries.why_it_matters: It emphasizes reliability and self-evolving skills, allowing agents to learn from feedback and adapt over time, contrasting with agents requiring constant manual refinement.

entries.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
entries.summary: The Microsoft Agent Framework is an open, multi-language framework for building and orchestrating production-grade AI agents and multi-agent workflows.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a consistent foundation for agent systems across Python and .NET, supporting various LLM ecosystems for developers taking agents from prototype to production.

entries.name: OpenHands
entries.summary: An open-source platform for autonomous AI software development agents capable of modifying code, running commands, and browsing the web.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides an open-source option for builders seeking autonomous coding agents, offering capabilities similar to proprietary solutions like Devin.

entries.name: CrewAI
entries.summary: CrewAI is a framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents, designed to simplify multi-agent teamwork and collaborative intelligence.
entries.why_it_matters: Unlike single-agent frameworks, CrewAI focuses on enabling seamless coordination among multiple agents to tackle complex tasks, streamlining multi-agent system development.

entries.name: Dify
entries.summary: Dify is a low-code platform offering an all-in-one toolchain for building, deploying, and managing AI applications with agentic and retrieval-augmented workflows.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a visual workflow builder to orchestrate tool-using agents, distinguishing it from code-centric agent frameworks by simplifying AI application development.

entries.name: CodeWhale
entries.summary: CodeWhale is a terminal-based AI coding agent designed for DeepSeek models, engineered for prefix-cache stability.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a specialized coding agent optimized for DeepSeek models, offering a stable terminal experience for developers.

entries.name: Open Design
entries.summary: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design, offering skills and design systems for generating various prototypes and media.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides an open-source, local alternative for AI-driven design generation, expanding options beyond proprietary tools like Claude Design.

entries.name: Google Antigravity
entries.summary: An agent-first development platform and IDE by Google for building and orchestrating autonomous AI agents across editor, terminal, and browser.
entries.why_it_matters: It shifts from traditional AI code assistance to a system where AI agents operate with greater autonomy, providing an abstracted UI for managing parallel subagents and scheduled tasks.

entries.name: OpenCode
entries.summary: OpenCode is an open-source, terminal-based AI coding agent that integrates with Git for commit-level code edits and supports over 100 languages.
entries.why_it_matters: Unlike commercial AI coding assistants, OpenCode offers a free, self-hosted option with broad language support and deep codebase context for developers.

entries.name: RAMPART (Microsoft)
entries.summary: RAMPART is a Pytest-native, open-source framework from Microsoft for testing the safety and security of AI agents during development.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a structured way to red-team AI agents early in the development lifecycle, complementing existing observability tools by focusing on security vulnerabilities.

entries.name: OpenClaw
entries.summary: OpenClaw is a free and open-source personal AI assistant that runs locally on users' devices, connecting AI models to numerous integrations.
entries.why_it_matters: It offers a privacy-focused, local-first approach to AI assistance with extensive integration capabilities and self-extending skill development, contrasting with cloud-dependent alternatives.

entries.name: Mistral Vibe
entries.summary: Mistral Vibe is a unified AI agent platform for work and coding, designed for long-running, multi-step tasks across various environments.
entries.why_it_matters: It integrates diverse productivity and coding functions into a single agent, enabling comprehensive automation of complex workflows beyond simple conversational AI.

entries.name: Velo
entries.summary: Velo is an AI video creation platform that transforms raw screen recordings into polished, shareable video messages with automated scripts and AI avatars.
entries.why_it_matters: It simplifies video production for demos and explainers by automating scriptwriting and avatar generation, reducing the need for extensive editing compared to traditional video tools.

entries.name: Kilo Code
entries.summary: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent for IDEs and CLI that generates code, automates tasks, and debugs across over 500 models.
entries.why_it_matters: It offers an open and flexible alternative to proprietary AI coding assistants by supporting a wide range of models and providing deep integration into developer workflows.

entries.name: DeepEval
entries.summary: DeepEval is an open-source, Python-first LLM evaluation framework that provides a pytest-native testing experience for AI applications.
entries.why_it_matters: It offers over 50 research-backed metrics for evaluating RAG, agents, and chatbots, enabling developers to integrate LLM evaluation into CI/CD workflows.

entries.name: Luma Dream Machine
entries.summary: Luma Dream Machine generates realistic, high-quality videos from text or image prompts, with capabilities like cinematic camera movements.
entries.why_it_matters: It offers native 1080p output and a Draft Mode for previewing low-resolution shots, which helps creators iterate efficiently and save resources.

entries.name: Markitdown (Microsoft)
entries.summary: A lightweight Python tool that converts various document formats like PDFs, Word, and Excel into clean Markdown for AI processing.
entries.why_it_matters: This tool simplifies preparing unstructured data for AI, enabling coding agents to more effectively analyze and summarize content compared to feeding raw, complex document formats. It addresses a common data preparation bottleneck for AI builders.

entries.name: CoreMem
entries.summary: CoreMem allows users to build and share portable collections of context, or 'mems,' with any AI agent via URL, extensions, or the Model Context Protocol.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a persistent and shareable memory layer, solving the problem of AI agents losing context across different sessions or when switching between various tools. This is crucial for building reliable and consistent agentic workflows.

entries.name: Rayfin
entries.summary: An open-source SDK and CLI that enables developers and coding agents to define and deploy complete application backends in code.
entries.why_it_matters: It simplifies backend development by automating database, authentication, and API provisioning, allowing rapid movement from prototype to production with enterprise-grade security on Microsoft Fabric.

entries.name: SubQ (by Subquadratic)
entries.summary: A large language model built on a fully sub-quadratic sparse-attention architecture, offering a native 12 million token context window.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides significantly increased context windows and faster inference at a much lower cost than traditional transformer-based models, making long-context reasoning practical for agents.

entries.name: SwarmClaw
entries.summary: SwarmClaw is an open-source, self-hosted runtime and multi-agent framework for orchestrating autonomous AI agent swarms with memory and skills.
entries.why_it_matters: It provides a comprehensive platform for deploying and managing complex multi-agent systems, offering a self-hosted alternative to other multi-agent frameworks.

entries.name: Headroom
entries.summary: Headroom compresses agent tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks to reduce LLM token usage while maintaining response quality.
entries.why_it_matters: It optimizes token efficiency for agents, which can significantly lower costs and improve performance compared to sending uncompressed data to LLMs.
